prevalence.trend: stable; recognition increasing in specialized services

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated self-harm and comorbidity risk

vulnerablePopulations: patients with complex PTSD and dissociative symptoms
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 dissociative identity disorder criteria with identity disruption, amnesia, and clinically significant distress/impairment; requires specialist longitudinal assessment

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; best outcomes with long-term trauma-focused psychotherapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Typically diagnosed in specialized psychiatry/psychology services; APS often identifies comorbid depression/PTSD first
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Long-term trauma-focused psychotherapy with stabilization, integration, and safety planning; treat comorbid depression/anxiety; crisis support as needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to specialized trauma psychotherapy and high stigma around severe mental illness
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS and psychosocial services can provide longitudinal support when available
